title.ko: 디지털 정보의 전송, 예. 전신통신(전신(telegraphic) 및 전화통신의 공통장치 H04M)
title.en: TRANSMISSION OF DIGITAL INFORMATION, e.g. TELEGRAPHIC COMMUNICATION (arrangements common to telegraphic and telephonic communication H04M)

definition.ko: 다음에 의해서 특징지어지는 것 사용되는 부호: 모스(Morse) 부호; 보우드(Baudot) 부호; 세부 H04L15/00; H04L17/00; H04L13/00 그 외: 스텝 바이 스텝; 모자이크 인쇄기; 그 외 시스템 H04L19/00; H04L21/00; H04L23/00 기저대역(baseband) 시스템 H04L25/00 변조전송 시스템 H04L27/00 데이터 스위칭 네트워크 H04L12/00 일반적 응용 장치 암호화; 보안, 오류; 네트워크 보안 H04L1/00; H04L9/00 다중통신; 동기화 H04L5/00; H04L7/00 데이터 교환 네트워크의 유지, 관리 또는 운영을 위한 장치 H04L41/00 데이터 교환 네트워크 모니터링 또는 테스트를 위한 장치 H04L43/00 데이터 교환 네트워크에서 패킷의 라우팅 또는 경로 탐색 H04L45/00 데이터 교환 네트워크의 트래픽 제어 H04L47/00 패킷 스위칭 요소 H04L49/00 패킷-스위칭 네트워크의 사용자 간(user-to-user) 메시징, 저장 후 전달(store-and-forward) 또는 실시간 프로토콜에 따라 전송되는 것 H04L51/00 어드레싱 또는 네이밍을 위한 네트워크 장치, 프로토콜 또는 서비스 H04L61/00 데이터 패킷 통신에서 실시간 애플리케이션을 지원하기 위한 네트워크 장치, 프로토콜 또는 서비스 H04L65/00 네트워크 서비스 또는 애플리케이션을 지원하기 위한 네트워크 장치 또는 프로토콜 H04L67/00 기타 장치, 기구 또는 시스템 H04L69/00

title.en: Arrangements for detecting or preventing errors in the information received
by diversity reception
using frequency diversity
using space diversity
by repeating transmission, e.g. Verdan system
by using return channel
in which the signals are sent back to the transmitter to be checked
in which the return channel carries supervisory signals, e.g. repetition request signals
Details of the supervisory signal
Automatic repetition systems, e.g. Van Duuren systems
Stop-and-wait protocols
Go-back-N protocols
Selective-repeat protocols
Hybrid protocols; Hybrid automatic repeat request [HARQ]
involving configuration of automatic repeat request [ARQ] with parallel processes
Adaptation of specific ARQ protocol parameters according to transmission conditions
Arrangements specially adapted for the receiver end
Arrangements specially adapted for the transmitter end
using signal-quality detector
using redundant apparatus to increase reliability
Testing correct operation

title.ko: 전송경로의 다중사용을 가능하게 하기 위한 배치
title.en: Arrangements affording multiple use of the transmission path

title.ko: 채널특성이 신호의 형식에 의한 것
title.en: Channels characterised by the type of signal
the signals being represented by different amplitudes or polarities, e.g. quadriplex

title.ko: 각 신호가 상이한 주파수에 의하여 표시되는 것 (시분할 다중시스템과 결합한 것 H04L 5/26)
title.en: the signals being represented by different frequencies (combined with time-division multiplexing H04L0005260000)
each combination of signals in different channels being represented by a fixed frequency
with dynamo-electric generation of carriers; with mechanical filters or demodulators
the signals being represented by different phase modulations of a single carrier
Two-way operation using the same type of signal, i.e. duplex
Half-duplex systems; Simplex/duplex switching; Transmission of break signals
Automatic changing of the traffic direction
using different combinations of lines, e.g. phantom working
using time-division multiplexing
with start-stop synchronous converters
combined with the use of different frequencies
Arrangements for synchronising receiver with transmitter
Speed or phase control by the received code signals, the signals containing no special synchronisation information
extracting the synchronising or clock signal from the received signal spectrum, e.g. by using a resonant or bandpass circuit
using the transitions of the received signal to control the phase of the synchronising-signal- generating means, e.g. using a phase-locked loop
Speed or phase control by synchronisation signals
the synchronisation signals differing from the information signals in amplitude, polarity, or frequency
the synchronisation signals recurring cyclically
Arrangements for initial synchronisation

title.ko: 비밀 또는 보안 통신을 위한 장치; 네트워크 보안 프로토콜
title.en: Arrangements for secret or secure communications; Network security protocols

definition.ko: 그룹 H04L9/06 ~ H04L9/32에서, 최후개소 우선 규칙이 적용됩니다. 즉. 각 계층 레벨에서 반대의 표시가 없는 경우 마지막 적절한 개소에 분류됩니다.

title.ko: 블럭적 부호화(blockwise coding)를 위한 시프트레지스터 또는 메모리를 사용하는 암호화 장치, 예. D. E. S 시스템
title.en: the encryption apparatus using shift registers or memories for blockwise coding, e.g. D.E.S. systems
Key distribution
with particular housing, physical features or manual controls
Transmitting and receiving encryption devices synchronised or initially set up in a particular manner
using a plurality of keys or algorithms
the keys or algorithms being changed during operation
Encryption by serially and continuously modifying data stream elements, e.g. stream cipher systems
Pseudorandom key sequence combined element-for-element with data sequence
with particular pseudorandom sequence generator
sequence produced by more than one generator
producing a nonlinear pseudorandom sequence
using particular encryption algorithm
Public key, i.e. encryption algorithm being computationally infeasible to invert and users' encryption keys not requiring secrecy
including means for verifying the identity or authority of a user of the system
Bits, or blocks of bits, of the telegraphic message being interchanged in time
with means for detecting characters not meant for transmission
Encryption being effected by mechanical apparatus, e.g. rotating cams, switches, keytape punchers
Network security protocols

title.ko: 데이터 스위칭 네트웍(기억장치들, 입출력장치, 중앙처리장치사이의 정보 혹은 다른 신호의 상호연결 혹은 전송 G06F 13/00)
title.en: Data switching networks (interconnection of, or transfer of information or other signals between, memories, input/output devices or central processing units G06F0013000000)

title.ko: 세부
title.en: Details
Switchboards
Answer-back mechanisms or circuits
Allotting numbers to messages; Counting characters, words or messages
Current supply arrangements
Arrangements for remote connection or disconnection of substations or of equipment thereof
Charging, metering or billing arrangements specially adapted for data communications, e.g. authentication, authorisation and accounting [AAA] framework
Arrangements for providing special services to substations
for broadcast or conference
for converting transmission speed from the inherent speed of a substation to the inherent speed of other substations

title.ko: 허가 없이 데이터 전송 채널로부터 데이터를 빼앗는 것을 방지하기 위한 장치 (보안 또는 비밀 통신 시스템의 사용자의 신원 또는 권한을 확인하기 위한 수단 H04L9/32)
title.en: Arrangements for preventing the taking of data from a data transmission channel without authorisation (means for verifying the identity or the authority of a user of a secure or secret communication system H04L0009320000)

title.ko: 경로구성을 특징으로 하는 것, 예. LAN[Local Area Network] 또는 WAN[Wide Area Network] (무선통신네트워크 H04W)
title.en: characterised by path configuration, e.g. LAN [Local Area Networks] or WAN [Wide Area Networks] (wireless communication networks H04W)
Bus networks
with centralised control, e.g. polling
with decentralised control
with random access, e.g. carrier-sense multiple-access with collision detection [CSMA-CD]
with deterministic access, e.g. token passing
Loop networks
with centralised control, e.g. polling
with decentralised control
with synchronous transmission, e.g. time division multiplex [TDM], slotted rings
with asynchronous transmission, e.g. token ring, register insertion
Ring fault isolation or reconfiguration
Star or tree networks
Interconnection of networks

title.ko: 서킷(Circuit) 스위칭 시스템, 예, 통신중에 경로가 물리적으로 고정된 시스템
title.en: Circuit switching systems, i.e. systems in which the path is physically permanent during the communication

title.ko: 시분할기술을 사용하는 것(디지털 전송 시스템에서 H04L 5/22)
title.en: using time division techniques (in digital transmission systems H04L0005220000)

title.ko: 축적 전송 스위칭 시스템 [store-and-forward switching system] (패킷 스위칭 시스템 H04L45/00, H04L47/00)
title.en: Store-and-forward switching systems (packet switching systems H04L0045000000, H04L0047000000)
Hybrid switching systems
Arrangements for connecting between networks having differing types of switching systems, e.g. gateways

title.ko: 그룹 15/00 또는 17/00에 포함되는 장치 또는 회로의 세부
title.en: Details of the apparatus or circuits covered by groups H04L0015000000 or H04L0017000000
Details not particular to receiver or transmitter
Driving mechanisms; Clutches
Tape or page guiding or feeding devices
Intermediate storage means
Distributors
Non-mechanical distributors, e.g. relay distributors
Electronic distributors
of transmitters, e.g. code-bars, code-discs
of receivers

title.ko: 도트 대시(dot and dash) 코드를 송신 또는 수신하기 위한 장치 또는 국부회로 예. 모르스 코드(morse code)(그를 위한 교습장치 G09B; 전신용 태핑 키(tapping key) H01H 21/86)
title.en: Apparatus or local circuits for transmitting or receiving dot-and-dash codes, e.g. Morse code (teaching apparatus therefor G09B;telegraph tapping keys H01H0021860000)
Keys structurally combined with sound generators
Apparatus or circuits at the transmitting end
with a restricted number of keys, e.g. separate key for each type of code element
with a single key which transmits dots in one position and dashes in a second position
combined with perforating apparatus
with keyboard co-operating with code-bars
combined with perforating apparatus
with keyboard co-operating with code discs
Automatic transmitters, e.g. controlled by perforated tape
with optical sensing means
Apparatus or circuits for sending one or a restricted number of signals, e.g. distress signals
Apparatus or circuits at the receiving end
operating only on reception of predetermined code signals, e.g. distress signals, party-line call signals
Code reproducing apparatus
Writing recorders
Perforating recorders
Apparatus for recording received coded signals after translation, e.g. as type-characters
Apparatus or local circuits for transmitting or receiving codes wherein each character is represented by the same number of equal-length code elements, e.g. Baudot code
Apparatus or circuits at the transmitting end
with keyboard co-operating with code-bars
Contact operating means
combined with perforating apparatus
with keyboard co-operating with code-discs
Automatic transmitters, e.g. controlled by perforated tape
with optical sensing means
Apparatus or circuits at the receiving end
Code selection mechanisms
using perforating recorders
using mechanical translation and type-bar printing
using mechanical translation and type-head printing, e.g. type-wheel, type-cylinder
using aggregate motion translation
using pneumatic or hydraulic translation
using electric or electronic translation
Apparatus or local circuits for step-by-step systems
Apparatus or local circuits for mosaic printer telegraph systems
at the transmitting end
at the receiving end

title.ko: 그룹 H04L 15/00내지 H04L 21/00에 포함되는 시스템이외의 시스템을 위한 장치 또는 국부회로
title.en: Apparatus or local circuits for telegraphic systems other than those covered by groups H04L0015000000-H04L0021000000
adapted for orthogonal signalling

title.ko: 기저대역 시스템
title.en: Baseband systems
Details
Shaping networks in transmitter or receiver, e.g. adaptive shaping networks
Passive shaping networks
Electric or magnetic storage of signals before transmitting or retransmitting for changing the transmission rate
DC level restoring means; Bias distortion correction
Modifications for reducing interference; Modifications for reducing effects due to line faults
Compensating for variations in line balance
Compensating for variations in line impedance
Channel dividing arrangements
Interpolating arrangements
Arrangements for inductively generating telegraphic signals
Repeater circuits; Relay circuits
Repeaters for converting two wires to four wires; Repeaters for converting single current to double current
Relay circuits using discharge tubes or semiconductor devices
Circuits with optical sensing means
Repeaters using modulation and subsequent demodulation
Non-synchronous systems
characterised by the code employed
using three or more different amplitudes, e.g. cable code

title.ko: 동기 또는 시작 정지시스템, 예. 보우드(baudot) 코드
title.en: Synchronous or start-stop systems, e.g. for Baudot code

title.ko: 송신회로; 수신회로
title.en: Transmitting circuits; Receiving circuits
using mechanical distributors
using relay distributors
using electronic distributors
using tuning forks or vibrating reeds

title.ko: 사용되는 부호에 의해서 특징지어지는 것(H04L 25/49가 우선)
title.en: characterised by the code employed (H04L0025490000 takes precedence)
using code conversion at the transmitter; using predistortion; using insertion of idle bits for obtaining a desired frequency spectrum; using three or more amplitude levels
by transition coding, i.e. the time-position or direction of a transition being encoded before transmission
by correlative coding, e.g. partial response coding or echo modulation coding
Repeater circuits; Relay circuits
using mechanical distributors
Non-electrical regenerative repeaters
using relay distributors
Regenerative repeaters with electromagnetic switches
using tuning forks or vibrating reeds
Start-stop regenerative repeaters using discharge tubes or semiconductor devices
Synchronous repeaters using discharge tubes or semiconductor devices

title.ko: 변조 전송시스템
title.en: Modulated-carrier systems
Equalisers

title.ko: 진폭-변조 반송파 시스템, 예. 온-오프 키잉을 사용하는 것; 단측파대 또는 잔류측파대 변조 (H04L27/32가 우선)
title.en: Amplitude-modulated carrier systems, e.g. using on/off keying; Single sideband or vestigial sideband modulation (H04L0027320000 takes precedence)
Modulator circuits; Transmitter circuits
Demodulator circuits; Receiver circuits
Amplitude regulation arrangements

title.ko: 주파수-변조 반송파 시스템, 즉. 주파수-시프트 키잉을 사용하는 것 (H04L27/32가 우선)
title.en: Frequency-modulated carrier systems, i.e. using frequency-shift keying (H04L0027320000 takes precedence)
Modulator circuits; Transmitter circuits
Demodulator circuits; Receiver circuits
with demodulation using spectral properties of the received signal, e.g. by using frequency selective- or frequency sensitive elements
using filters, including PLL-type filters
using controlled oscillators, e.g. PLL arrangements
with demodulation using temporal properties of the received signal, e.g. detecting pulse width
Frequency regulation arrangements

title.ko: 위상-변조 반송파 시스템, 즉. 위상-편이 키잉을 사용하는 것 (H04L27/32가 우선)
title.en: Phase-modulated carrier systems, i.e. using phase-shift keying (H04L0027320000 takes precedence)
Modulator circuits; Transmitter circuits
Demodulator circuits; Receiver circuits
using coherent demodulation
using non-coherent demodulation
Half-wave signalling systems

title.ko: 다주파부호를 이용하는 시스템(H04L 27/32가 우선)
title.en: Systems using multi-frequency codes (H04L0027320000 takes precedence)
with simultaneous transmission of different frequencies each representing one code element
wherein each code element is represented by a combination of frequencies

title.ko: 그룹 H04L 27/02, H04L 27/10, H04L 27/18 또는 H04L 27/26에 포함되는 두개 이상의 결합에 의하여 특징지어지는 반송시스템
title.en: Carrier systems characterised by combinations of two or more of the types covered by groups H04L0027020000, H04L0027100000, H04L0027180000, or H04L0027260000
Amplitude- and phase-modulated carrier systems, e.g. quadrature-amplitude modulated carrier systems
Modulator circuits; Transmitter circuits
Demodulator circuits; Receiver circuits

title.ko: 데이터 스위칭 네트워크의 유지 또는 관리를 위한 장치, 예. 패킷 스위칭 네트워크
title.en: Arrangements for maintenance, administration or management of data switching networks, e.g. of packet switching networks

title.ko: 표준화; 통합
title.en: Standardisation; Integration
Standardised network management protocols, e.g. simple network management protocol [SNMP]
Multivendor or multi-standard integration
Mapping or translating multiple network management protocols
Object-oriented techniques, for representation of network management data, e.g. common object request broker architecture [CORBA]

title.ko: 인터넷을 사용한 네트워크 관리 정보 교환 또는 전송; 네트워크 관리 웹서버를 내장한 네트워크 요소; 웹서비스 기반 프로토콜
title.en: Exchanging or transporting network management information using the Internet; Embedding network management web servers in network elements; Web-services-based protocols
using browsers or web-pages for accessing management information

title.ko: 관리 정보 전송을 위해 전자 메세징을 포함하는 것, 예. 이메일, 인스턴트 메세징 또는 채팅
title.en: using e-messaging for transporting management information, e.g. email, instant messaging or chat
using meta-data, objects or commands for formatting management information, e.g. using eXtensible markup language [XML]
using web services for network management, e.g. simple object access protocol [SOAP]
Network management architectures or arrangements
comprising distributed management centres cooperatively managing the network
comprising hierarchical management structures
comprising client-server management architectures
comprising network management agents or mobile agents therefor
using standardised network management architectures, e.g. telecommunication management network [TMN] or unified network management architecture [UNMA]

title.ko: 장애, 이벤트, 알람 또는 알림의 관리
title.en: Management of faults, events, alarms or notifications
using filtering, e.g. reduction of information by using priority, element types, position or time
using root cause analysis; using analysis of correlation between notifications, alarms or events based on decision criteria, e.g. hierarchy, tree or time analysis

title.ko: 네트워크 장애 복구를 사용 (네트워크 관리 시스템의 복구 액션 없이 링 오류 격리 또는 루프 네트워크 재설정 H04L12/437)
title.en: using network fault recovery (ring fault isolation or reconfiguration in loop networks without recovery actions by a network management system H04L0012437000)
by isolating or reconfiguring faulty entities
Performing the actions predefined by failover planning, e.g. switching to standby network elements
by dynamic selection of recovery network elements, e.g. replacement by the most appropriate element after failure
Localisation of faults
Configuration of triggering conditions
Additional information in the notification, e.g. enhancement of specific meta-data
using logs of notifications; Post-processing of notifications
the faulty arrangement being the maintenance, administration or management system

title.ko: 네트워크 또는 네트워크 요소의 설정 관리 (어드레스 할당 H04L61/50)
title.en: Configuration management of networks or network elements (address allocation H04L0061500000)

title.ko: 구성 설정 [Configuration setting]
title.en: Configuration setting
for initial configuration or provisioning, e.g. plug-and-play
characterised by the conditions triggering a change of settings
the condition being an adaptation, e.g. in response to network events
the condition being updates or upgrades of network functionality

title.ko: 설정 변경의 목적을 특징으로 하는 것, 예. 신뢰성 향상을 위한 설정 최적화 (무선 네트워크의 동작 설정 최적화를 위한 것 H04W24/02)
title.en: characterised by the purposes of a change of settings, e.g. optimising configuration for enhancing reliability (for optimising operational conditions of wireless networks H04W0024020000)

title.ko: 네트워크 비용 감소를 위한 (H04L41/0833이 우선)
title.en: for reduction of network costs (H04L0041083300 takes precedence)
for increasing network speed
for reduction of network energy consumption
Configuration by using pre-existing information, e.g. using templates or copying from other elements
Retrieval of network configuration; Tracking network configuration history
by actively collecting configuration information or by backing up configuration information
by keeping history of different configuration generations or by rolling back to previous configuration versions
Checking the configuration
Validating the configuration within one network element
Checking configuration conflicts between network elements
Assignment of logical groups to network elements
Policy-based network configuration management
Configuration of virtualised networks or elements, e.g. virtualised network function or OpenFlow elements

title.ko: 대역폭 또는 용량 관리, 즉. 용량의 자동 증가 또는 감소 (동적 자원 할당을 사용한 흐름 또는 혼잡 제어, 예. 통화 중 재협상, H04L47/76)
title.en: Bandwidth or capacity management, i.e. automatically increasing or decreasing capacities   (flow or congestion control using dynamic resource allocation, e.g. in-call renegotiation, H04L0047760000)
by horizontal or vertical scaling of resources, or by migrating entities, e.g. virtual resources or entities
Discovery or management of network topologies
of virtualised topologies e.g. software-defined networks [SDN] or network function virtualisation [NFV]
Network analysis or design
using statistical or mathematical methods
for predicting network behaviour
for prediction of maintenance
using machine learning or artificial intelligence
Delegation of network management function, e.g. customer network management [CNM]
comprising specially adapted graphical user interfaces [GUI]
Restricting access to network management systems or functions, e.g. using authorisation function to access network configuration
Signalling channels for network management communication
between virtual entities, e.g. orchestrators, SDN or NFV entities
Out-of-band transfers
using virtualisation of network functions or resources, e.g. SDN or NFV entities
Network service management, e.g. ensuring proper service fulfilment according to agreements
Managing SLA; Interaction between SLA and QoS
Creating or negotiating SLA contracts, guarantees or penalties
Determining service level performance parameters or violations of service level contracts, e.g. violations of agreed response time or mean time between failures [MTBF]
Ensuring fulfilment of SLA
by giving priorities, e.g. assigning classes of service
by proactively reacting to service quality change, e.g. by reconfiguration after service quality degradation or upgrade
characterised by the time relationship between creation and deployment of a service
Service on demand, e.g. definition and deployment of services in real time
Automatic deployment of services triggered by the service manager, e.g. service implementation by automatic configuration of network components
characterised by the interaction between service providers and their network customers, e.g. customer relationship management
Customer-centric QoS measurements
Filtering out customers affected by service problems
Handling of user complaints or trouble tickets
Arrangements for monitoring or testing data switching networks
Capturing of monitoring data
by sampling
by adaptive sampling
using flow identification
by filtering
Processing captured monitoring data, e.g. for logfile generation
for graphical visualisation of monitoring data
Generation of reports
related to network traffic
related to network devices
using time frame reporting
Monitoring or testing based on specific metrics, e.g. QoS, energy consumption or environmental parameters
by checking availability
by checking connectivity
by checking functioning

title.en: Peer-to-peer [P2P] networks
using topology management mechanisms

title.ko: 노드 기반 피어 검색 메커니즘을 사용하는 (복제된 서버에 대한 정적 액세스 H04L67/1006, 서비스 검색 H04L67/51)
title.en: using node-based peer discovery mechanisms (static access to replicated servers H04L0067100600;service discovery H04L0067510000)

title.ko: 데이터 블록 전송 메커니즘을 지원하기 위한 (파일 전송 H04L67/06)
title.en: for supporting data block transmission mechanisms (file transfer H04L0067060000)
using cross-functional networking aspects
Replication or mirroring of data, e.g. scheduling or transport for data synchronisation between network nodes
for distributed storage of data in networks, e.g. transport arrangements for network file system [NFS], storage area networks [SAN] or network attached storage [NAS]
specially adapted for proprietary or special-purpose networking environments, e.g. medical networks, sensor networks, networks in vehicles or remote metering networks
involving control of end-device applications over a network
Protocols for games, networked simulations or virtual reality
Protocols for remote procedure calls [RPC]
specially adapted for monitoring users’ activity

title.ko: 세션 관리 (데이터 패킷 통신 네트워크의 실시간 애플리케이션의 경우 H04L65/1066)
title.en: Session management (for real-time applications in data packet communications networks H04L0065106600)

title.ko: 어플리케이션 세션 설정 (데이터 스위칭 네트워크에서 수락 제어 또는 자원 할당 H04L47/70)
title.en: Setup of application sessions (admission control or resource allocation in data switching networks H04L0047700000)
Managing session states for stateless protocols; Signalling session states; State transitions; Keeping-state mechanisms
Termination or inactivation of sessions, e.g. event-controlled end of session
avoiding end of session, e.g. keep-alive, heartbeats, resumption message or wake-up for inactive or interrupted session
Markers for unambiguous identification of a particular session, e.g. session cookie or URL-encoding
Signalling methods or messages providing extensions to protocols defined by standardisation
Migration or transfer of sessions
Architectures; Arrangements
Terminals specially adapted for communication
Implementation details of single intermediate entities
Pairs of inter-processing entities at each side of the network, e.g. split proxies
Distributed intermediate devices, i.e. intermediate devices for interaction with other intermediate devices on the same level
Hierarchically arranged intermediate devices, e.g. for hierarchical caching
Intermediate processing functionally located close to the data consumer application, e.g. in same machine, in same home or in same sub-network
Intermediate processing functionally located close to the data provider application, e.g. reverse proxies
Profiles
Terminal profiles
User profiles

title.ko: 네트워크 서비스
title.en: Network services
Discovery or management thereof, e.g. service location protocol [SLP] or web services
specially adapted for the location of the user terminal
using third party service providers
Presence management, e.g. monitoring or registration for receipt of user log-on information, or the connection status of the users
Push-based network services

title.ko: 프록시 서비스의 프로비저닝 (데이터 스위칭 네트워크의 스토어 앤 포워드(store-and-forward) 스위칭 시스템 H04L12/54)
title.en: Provisioning of proxy services (store-and-forward switching systems in data switching networks H04L0012540000)
Adding application-functional data or data for application control, e.g. adding metadata
Brokering proxy services
Data redirection of data network streams
Enhancement of application control based on intercepted application data

title.ko: 어플리케이션 형식 또는 콘텐츠의 변환 또는 적응 (어플리케이션 제어 또는 어플리케이션 기능 데이터 추가 H04L67/561)
title.en: Conversion or adaptation of application format or content  (adding application control or application functional data H04L0067561000)
Reducing the amount or size of exchanged application data
Grouping or aggregating service requests, e.g. for unified processing
Integrating service provisioning from a plurality of service providers
Storing data temporarily at an intermediate stage, e.g. caching
Pre-fetching or pre-delivering data based on network characteristics
Policies or rules for updating, deleting or replacing the stored data
Storage of data provided by user terminals, i.e. reverse caching
Providing operational support to end devices by off-loading in the network or by emulation, e.g. when they are unavailable

title.ko: 어플리케이션 요청의 서비스 스케줄링 또는 구성, 예. 필요한 네트워크 리소스의 분석 및 최적화를 사용하여 어플리케이션 데이터 전송 요청 (수락 제어 또는 자원 할당 H04L47/70)
title.en: Scheduling or organising the servicing of application requests, e.g. requests for application data transmissions using the analysis and optimisation of the required network resources (admission control or resource allocation H04L0047700000)
taking into account QoS or priority requirements
Establishing a time schedule for servicing the requests
Routing a service request depending on the request content or context
Indicating network or usage conditions on the user display

title.ko: 어플리케이션 페이로드와 독립적이고, 이 서브클래스의 다른 그룹이 제공하지 않는 네트워크 장치, 프로토콜 또는 서비스 (네트워크 보안 프로토콜 H04L9/40, 무선 통신 네트워크 H04W)
title.en: Network arrangements, protocols or services independent of the application payload and not provided for in the other groups of this subclass (networks security protocols H04L0009400000;wireless communication networks H04W)
Protocols for data compression, e.g. ROHC
Notations for structuring of protocol data, e.g. abstract syntax notation one [ASN.1]
Protocols for interworking; Protocol conversion
specially adapted for interworking of IP-based networks with other networks
Streamlined, light-weight or high-speed protocols, e.g. express transfer protocol [XTP] or byte stream
Protocol engines
Multichannel or multilink protocols

title.ko: 전송 제어 프로토콜 [TCP] 또는 사용자 데이터그램 프로토콜 [UDP]의 인터넷 프로토콜 [IP]의 구현 또는 적응
title.en: Implementation or adaptation of Internet protocol [IP], of transmission control protocol [TCP] or of user datagram protocol [UDP]
In-band adaptation of TCP data exchange; In-band control procedures
Adaptation or special uses of UDP protocol
Combined use of TCP and UDP protocols; selection criteria therefor
IP fragmentation; TCP segmentation

title.ko: 두 개의 IP 버전 간의 전환을 위한 적응, 예. IPv4와 IPv6 사이 (인터넷 프로토콜 [IP] 주소 변환 H04L61/2503)
title.en: Adaptation for transition between two IP versions, e.g. between IPv4 and IPv6 (translation of Internet protocol [IP] addresses H04L0061250300)
specially adapted for link layer protocols, e.g. asynchronous transfer mode [ATM], synchronous optical network [SONET] or point-to-point protocol [PPP]
Multiprotocol handlers, e.g. single devices capable of handling multiple protocols
Parsing or analysis of headers
Negotiation of communication capabilities
Timers or timing mechanisms used in protocols

title.ko: 계층화된 프로토콜 스택의 정의, 표준 또는 아키텍처 측면
title.en: Definitions, standards or architectural aspects of layered protocol stacks

title.ko: 개방형 시스템 상호 연결 [OSI] 7 계층 프로토콜 스택, 예. 데이터 링크 레벨과 물리 레벨간의 인터페이스
title.en: Architecture of open systems interconnection [OSI] 7-layer type protocol stacks, e.g. the interfaces between the data link level and the physical level
Interlayer communication protocols or service data unit [SDU] definitions; Interfaces between layers

title.ko: 피어 엔티티 또는 프로토콜 데이터 유닛 [PDU] 정의 중의 인트라-계층 (intra layer)통신 프로토콜 측면
title.en: Intralayer communication protocols among peer entities or protocol data unit [PDU] definitions
in the physical layer [OSI layer 1]
in the data link layer [OSI layer 2], e.g. HDLC

title.ko: 네트워크 계층 [OSI계층 3]에서, 예. X.25 (H04L69/16가 우선)
title.en: in the network layer [OSI layer 3], e.g. X.25 (H04L0069160000 takes precedence)

title.ko: 전송층 [OSI계층 4] (H04L69/16가 우선)
title.en: in the transport layer [OSI layer 4] (H04L0069160000 takes precedence)
in the session layer [OSI layer 5]
in the presentation layer [OSI layer 6]
in the application layer [OSI layer 7]

title.ko: 프로토콜 인스턴스 또는 엔티티의 실패로 부터 복구하기 위한 것, 예. 서비스 중복 프로토콜, 프로토콜 상태 중복 또는 프로토콜 서비스 리디렉션 (데이터 전환 네트워크의 오류, 이벤트, 경보 또는 알림 관리 H04L41/06)
title.en: for recovering from a failure of a protocol instance or entity, e.g. service redundancy protocols, protocol state redundancy or protocol service redirection (management of faults, events, alarms or notifications in data switching networks H04L0041060000)

title.ko: 그룹 H04L61/00에 관련된 인덱싱 체계
title.en: Indexing scheme associated with group H04L0061000000

title.ko: 그룹 H04L61/00에 관련된 인덱싱 체계
title.en: Indexing scheme associated with group H04L0061000000

title.ko: 네트워크 이름의 유형
title.en: Types of network names
containing non-Latin characters, e.g. Chinese domain names
containing protocol addresses or telephone numbers
containing wildcard characters
containing special prefixes
containing special suffixes
Application layer names, e.g. buddy names, unstructured names chosen by a user or home appliance name

title.en: E-mail addresses
Access point names [APN]
Telephone uniform resource identifier [URI]
Uniform resource identifier for session initiation protocol [SIP URI]
Globally routable user-agent uniform resource identifier [GRUU] for the session initiation protocol [SIP]
Internet protocol multimedia private identity [IMPI]; Internet protocol multimedia public identity [IMPU]
Types of network addresses
Address structures or formats
Details of network addresses
Layer-2 addresses, e.g. medium access control [MAC] addresses
Controller area network [CAN] identifiers
Small computer system interface [SCSI] addresses
IEEE1394 identification numbers
Asynchronous transfer mode [ATM] addresses
Fibre channel identifiers
Telephone numbers
International mobile subscriber identity [IMSI] numbers
Internet protocol version 6 [IPv6] addresses
Transport layer addresses, e.g. aspects of transmission control protocol [TCP] or user datagram protocol [UDP] ports
Internet protocol [IP] address subnets
Short addresses
Multiple interfaces, e.g. multihomed nodes
using addresses for wireless personal area networks or wireless sensor networks, e.g. Zigbee addresses
using dual-stack hosts, e.g. in Internet protocol version 4 [IPv4]/Internet protocol version 6 [IPv6] networks
